diff --git a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
@@ -23,10 +23,14 @@
 
 @router.post("/run", status_code=202)
 async def start_run(
     body: PipelineRunRequest,
     background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
 ) -> dict:
     """
     Start a new pipeline run in the background.
     """
+    # Input Validation Guard
+    if not body.diff_text or len(body.diff_text.strip()) < 10:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="diff_text must be at least 10 characters long.")
+        
     orchestrator = PipelineOrchestrator()
     # A temporary orchestration is needed to get the run_id before starting

scenarios.title: Successfully trigger a new pipeline run via API
scenarios.description: Verify that POST /api/pipeline/run accepts a valid PipelineRunRequest and returns a 202 status with a run_id.
scenarios.expected_result: API returns 202 Accepted and a unique run_id is generated for the background task.

scenarios.title: Monitor pipeline status via SSE stream
scenarios.description: Verify that the SSE endpoint /api/pipeline/status/{run_id} streams events for a newly created run.
preconditions: A pipeline run has been initiated and run_id is obtained
scenarios.expected_result: The client receives a stream of events tracking the progress of the pipeline run.

scenarios.title: Verify completed run appears in reports list
scenarios.description: Verify that after a run completes, it is persisted and visible in the reports summary.
preconditions: A pipeline run has completed (SUCCESS or FAILED)

steps.assertion: list contains the recently completed run_id

scenarios.expected_result: The report directory is updated and the new report is included in the list of all runs.

scenarios.title: Trigger pipeline with invalid request body
scenarios.description: Verify that POST /api/pipeline/run returns 422 Unprocessable Entity when the request body is malformed or missing required fields.
scenarios.expected_result: The API rejects the request with a validation error message.

scenarios.title: Access status for non-existent run_id
scenarios.description: Verify the behavior of the SSE endpoint when an invalid or non-existent run_id is provided.
scenarios.expected_result: The API returns a 404 Not Found error for non-existent pipeline runs.

scenarios.title: Concurrent pipeline run requests
scenarios.description: Ensure the system can handle multiple concurrent POST requests to /api/pipeline/run without race conditions in the orchestrator.
scenarios.expected_result: Multiple unique run_ids are generated and background tasks are queued correctly.

scenarios.title: Persistence of report on unexpected orchestrator error
scenarios.description: Verify that even if an unexpected exception occurs in the pipeline, the status is set to FAILED and the report is saved.
preconditions: A pipeline run is triggered that is designed to raise an unhandled Exception
scenarios.expected_result: The Orchestrator's try/except block catches the error, updates the status, and writes the JSON report.

scenarios.title: Injection probe in pipeline request fields
scenarios.description: Check if the API handles XSS or script injection attempts in the PipelineRunRequest fields.

steps.assertion: Content is sanitized or properly escaped in JSON response

scenarios.expected_result: The application does not execute injected scripts and persists data safely.

import os

from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

# Configuration

# SCENARIO_ID: 74715378-197b-4acd-a134-1e2abb453a80

# SCENARIO_ID: 277d4c9d-8893-49ad-8dfd-f7cd713615ba

    # This test depends on a valid run_id from the trigger scenario
    # TODO: resolve PipelineRunRequest
    pytest.skip("payload model not in context")

# SCENARIO_ID: d24025cc-4642-4e66-a74b-21e14e2dfb9b
def test_verify_completed_run_appears_in_reports_list(page: Page):
    reports_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports"
    
    # Use request API to verify the data structure
    response = page.request.get(reports_url)
    expect(response).to_be_ok()
    
    # Use navigation to verify the endpoint is accessible
    page.goto(reports_url)
    # JSON is typically rendered in a <pre> tag or as raw text in the body
    # We expect at least an empty list or a list of report objects
    body_text = page.locator("body")
    expect(body_text).to_contain_text("[")
    expect(body_text).to_contain_text("]")

# SCENARIO_ID: 42c5b691-3763-497d-a724-31c39ce1f12a
def test_trigger_pipeline_with_invalid_request_body(page: Page):
    run_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run"
    
    # Sending an empty object to trigger validation error (422 Unprocessable Entity)
    response = page.request.post(run_url, data={})
    
    assert response.status == 422
    error_detail = response.json()
    assert "detail" in error_detail

# SCENARIO_ID: d42bbf70-47c3-43cf-a0ae-b652a6d27e41
def test_access_status_for_non_existent_run_id(page: Page):
    invalid_run_id = "invalid-uuid-123"
    status_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/status/{invalid_run_id}"
    
    response = page.goto(status_url)
    
    # response might be None if navigation fails, but for 404 it should return the response
    assert response is not None
    assert response.status == 404
